channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Tampa Bay hasn’t been hit directly by a major hurricane since 1921. Milton may be the one
item.description: FORT MYERS BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Florida's Gulf Coast braced Tuesday for the impact of Hurricane Milton's winds and expected massive storm surge, which could bring destruction to areas already reeling from Helene's devastation 12 days ago and still recovering from Ian's wrath two years ago.
item.dc:creator: TERRY SPENCER and HAVEN DALEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Milton dips to Category 4 as Florida braces for impact
item.description: Florida's Gulf Coast braces for the impact of Hurricane Milton's winds and expected massive storm surge, which could bring destruction to areas already reeling from Helene's devastation 12 days ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gabby Petito describes Utah fight with Brian Laundrie in conversation with female park ranger: bodycam
item.description: A female US Park Ranger approached Gabby Petito during a stop in Utah involving her suspected killer Brian Laundrie in previously unreleased bodycam video.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: University of Michigan president's home spray-painted with anti-Israel messages: 'Intifada'
item.description: The home of the University of Michigan president was vandalized, with anti-Israel messages spray-painted on the home on the first anniversary of Hamas' attack against Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court to hear challenge to ghost-gun regulation
item.description: The Supreme Court is hearing a challenge to a Biden administration regulation on ghost guns, the difficult-to-trace weapons with an exponentially increased link to crime in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Diddy accuser’s lawyer: Combs used oil, knife
item.description: "My client was raped by Mr. Combs, his bodyguard, and friend who invited my client to his home to set up this whole situation.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Which students were most affected by 2024 FAFSA glitch?
item.description: FAFSA's summer issues — which included preventing parents without a Social Security number from filing — left many students scrambling.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: What polling shows about Black voters' views of Harris and Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Black registered voters have an overwhelmingly positive view of Vice President Kamala Harris, but they’re less sure that she would change the country for the better, according to a recent poll from the AP-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Korea's Kim again threatens to use nuclear weapons against South Korea and US
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un warned again that he could use nuclear weapons in potential conflicts with South Korea and the United States, as he accused them of provoking North Korea and raising animosities on the Korean Peninsula, state media reported Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Here's why experts are so worried about Hurricane Milton
item.description: Residents of the Sunshine State are no strangers to hurricanes, but experts are sounding the alarm over Milton.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Murdered Kansas moms: New charges filed in deaths of women on road trip
item.description: Three of the five people accused in the murder of two Kansas women earlier this year are charged with some new crimes and also had some charges dropped.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Raccoon sparks chaos in stands during Broncos game against Raiders
item.description: Football fans packed Empower Field in Denver on Sunday to watch the Broncos take on the Las Vegas Raiders, but they weren’t the only ones in attendance.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Hurricane Milton strengthens into a Category 5 as Florida prepares for evacuations
item.description: Milton rapidly strengthened in the Gulf of Mexico on Monday, becoming a Category 5 storm on a path toward Florida.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal court reviews civil rights lawsuit alleging environmental racism in a Louisiana parish
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A federal appellate court is reviewing a civil rights lawsuit alleging a south Louisiana parish engaged in racist land-use policies to place polluting industries in majority-Black communities. The Fifth U.S.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Illegal immigrant previously charged with raping 8-year-old girl arrested after erroneous release
item.description: ICE announced the arrest of a Honduran illegal alien who was previously charged with raping an 8-year-old girl in Baton Rouge, Louisiana, and erroneously released by local authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Social Security COLA for 2025 predicted to be lower than 2024
item.description: The Social Security Administration will announce a new cost-of-living adjustment on Oct. 10, 2024, with the increase expected to be smaller than last year.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, KRON4

item.title: Nation's largest water utility pauses billing amid cyberattack
item.description: The largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States announced Monday that it was the victim of a cyberattack, prompting the firm to pause billing to customers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: A peek inside human brain shows a way it cleans out waste
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A unique peek inside the human brain may help explain how it clears away waste like the kind that can build up and lead to Alzheimer’s disease. Brain cells use a lot of nutrients which means they make a lot of waste.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: These cities are the 'safest' in America, new study says
item.description: The quality of schools, traffic and entertainment offerings are among the variety of factors you consider before moving to a new city. Another is safety.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Villanova University student raped by Uber driver in dorm room, police say
item.description: A Villanova University student was allegedly sexually assaulted by her Uber driver last month after he dropped her off on campus after a short night out.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FEMA administrator continues pushback against false claims as Helene death toll hits 230
item.description: The head of the Federal Emergency Management Agency is again forcefully pushing back against false claims and conspiracy theories about how her agency is responding to Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: John Raby, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Titan's former lead engineer says he felt pressured to get the submersible ready
item.description: The lead engineer for an experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreck of the Titanic testified Monday that he felt pressured to get the vessel ready to dive and refused to pilot it for a journey several years earlier.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and LISA J. ADAMS WAGNER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Singer Cissy Houston, mother of Whitney Houston, dies at 91
item.description: Cissy Houston, the mother of the late Whitney Houston and a two-time Grammy winner who performed alongside superstar musicians like Elvis Presley, and Aretha Franklin, has died. She was 91.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump mass deportation plan embraced by some border sheriffs
item.description: Speaking exclusively to NewsNation last week, Trump detailed why he would utilize the expertise of local authorities for his deportation plans.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: James Taylor, Luke Combs, Eric Church to take part in Helene relief concert in NC
item.description: "Concert for Carolina," featuring North Carolina natives James Taylor, Luke Combs and Eric Church, will be held at Bank of America Stadium in Charlotte with all proceeds going to Hurricane Helene relief efforts across the Carolina region, organizers announced.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Ullmann, ABC 4

item.title: Many loathe public speakerphone calls, so why do people do it?
item.description: The fact that it's a phone conversation — not just in-person chit-chat — could be part of the reason people find loud speakerphone calls so grating, speculated Ozlem Ayduk.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court won't hear arguments in Texas emergency abortion case
item.description: The Supreme Court decided not to hear arguments in a case involving Texas that could have provided an answer about whether a state abortion ban conflicts with a federal emergency care law.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Trump: Israel has to 'get smart' about supporting him
item.description: Former President Trump said Monday Israel has to “get smart” about supporting him and suggested Jewish people have not adequately backed him after he took several pro-Israel actions while in office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Missing woman found dead in suitcase at Seattle homeless encampment
item.description: Police confirmed that a body found in a suitcase in a Seattle homeless encampment was 37-year-old Shannon Marie Caslin Reeder on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Are hurricanes intensifying more rapidly than ever before?
item.description: Rapid intensification is defined by the National Hurricane Center as an increase of at least 30 knots (about 35 mph) in maximum sustained winds within a 24-hour period, which can mean a jump of two categories on the Saffir-Simpson scale.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court won’t hear Pennsylvania legislators' challenge to executive actions expanding voting access
item.description: The Supreme Court decided Monday it won’t take up a case to restore a lawsuit by Pennsylvania Republicans that challenged executive actions expanding voting access on the basis that only state legislatures can regulate federal elections.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court takes the bench with ghost guns, a capital case and transgender rights on the docket
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court took the bench again on Monday, ready to hear cases on ghost guns, a death sentence and transgender rights.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man falls 200 feet to his death at Zion National Park
item.description: A man fell while canyoneering at Zion National Park on Saturday, resulting in his death, according to the NPS.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, NewsNation

item.title: Major North Carolina newspaper knocks Trump over Helene response 'falsehoods'
item.description: A leading newspaper in North Carolina is blasting former President Donald Trump over what it calls his "falsehoods" about the government response to Hurricane Helene, which devastated large swaths of the state last week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Colleges brace for Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: Colleges face a major test Monday as pro-Palestinian and pro-Israel students both hold events for the first anniversary of Hamas’s attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Another top aide to New York City mayor resigns amid federal probe
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Embattled New York City Mayor Eric Adams said Monday that he’s accepted the resignation of his deputy mayor for public safety, Philip Banks, the latest senior official to leave as the mayor fends off an indictment and calls to step down.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KAREN MATTHEWS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here's what Spirit Halloween seeks in an 'ideal' retail location
item.description: Spirit Halloween made headlines earlier this week after firing back at "SNL" over a sketch that mocked the company's choice of retail locations.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4
